feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance
feed.subtitle: SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: Identity Access Management (IAM) Reference Architectures 2024
entry.content:
Identity Access Management Reference Architectures in 2024

We are happy to share with you that we just released an update to our reference architectures (2024 version).

The latest version is published in SAP Discovery Center along with further links to our documentation and to related missions. We want to support you trying out easily what we describe.

If you are new to this topic, consider reading my older blog post about Cloud leading Identity Lifecycle from 2021. The 1st chapter is still valid to start with - although it's 3 years old 🙂

We have an updated version of the SAP Secure Operations Map which allows you to verify your security requirements and map them to the regional requirements like NIST or BSI.
The Secure Operations Map contains in the application layer the three main IAM pillars that are now described in the SAP Discovery Center:

Authentication flows


Authorization flows as part of the identity lifecycle

Identity Lifecycle flows

 

Please read them and we can use this community to discuss.

If you want to know more about the SAP Cloud Identity Services I recommend this blog post.

 

PS: Yes, we are already working on an integrated architecture which considers SAP Access Control - but we need a bit more time.

entry.title: [SF] (Single Sign-On) SSO for SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Introduction to Single Sign-On (SSO)

Single Sign-On (SSO) is a user authentication process that enables users to access multiple applications with a single set of login credentials. Implementing SSO for SAP SuccessFactors enhances security, reduces password fatigue, and improves user experience. This guide provides a detailed, step-by-step approach to configuring SSO using SAP Identity Authentication Service (IAS) and Azure Active Directory.

Prerequisites

Before starting the configuration, ensure you have the following:

  • Administrative access to SAP SuccessFactors, SAP Identity Authentication Service (IAS),

  • Administrative access to Azure Active Directory (Azure AD).

  • An understanding of SAML-based authentication.

Step-by-Step Configuration

  • IAS Tenant preparation: Log onto Identity Authentication service
  1.  Navigate to Identity provisioning > Source > Properties > sf.user.filter  make it (active eq "true")
  2. Navigate to Identity provisioning > Source > Outbound Certificates and make sure that the certificate is valid, if not generate a new one and delete the old one and activate the automatic generation
  3. Go to Identity provisioning > Target > Outbound Certificates and make sure that the certificate is valid, if not generate a new one and delete the old one and activate the automatic generation

Note: If the IAS tenant links were not provided from SAP, you can activate from the Upgrade Center, and after completing the configuration, testing and activation will be done again from the Upgrade Center

  • Created trust between Azure Active Directory and Identity Authentication service

Step 1: Download Identity Authentication service tenant metadata

Navigate to Applications and resources > Tenant Setting > Single Sign-On > SAML 2.0 Configuration and download the IAS Meta data file

Download the metadata file.

Step 2: Create enterprise application in Azure Active Directory

Navigate to the Enterprise applications, Click New application.

Azure Active Directory has templates for a variety of applications, one of them is the SAP Cloud Platform Identity Authentication Service. Search for this and select it.

A new column on the right side will appear to give the application a name. Give the application a name and click Add.

Go to Single sign-on and select SAML as Single-Sign On method.

 

 

 

 

 

STEP 3: Upload the IAS tenant metadata file you get from the step 1 

Select the application you just created, Click Upload metadata to upload the metadata file from Identity Authentication service.

All the details are now taken from the metadata file. There’s nothing to do for you other than saving the details. Therefore, click Save.

STEP 4: Download single sign-on metadata from Azure Active Directory

Download the federation metadata as shown below.

With this information we can setup the trust between Azure Active Directory and Identity Authentication service.

Step 5: Create corporate identity provider in IAS

Go back to IAS and navigate to Identity provider > Create > Microsoft ADFS / Entra AD (SAML 2.0) Type

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

STEP 6: Upload Azure Active Directory federation metadata file

Click SAML 2.0 Configuration and to upload the recently downloaded federation metadata from Azure Active Directory.

Choose the file from your local file system.

All fields below are automatically going to be filled due to the information provided through the uploaded file.

Click Save at the top of the page.

STEP 6: Add a new user in the Users and groups Microsoft Azure application

Go back to your overview of enterprise applications in Microsoft Azure AD and click your application. Add a new user by clicking Add user in the Users and groups submenu, as shown on the screenshot.

By hitting the result tile, you select the user, which should appear under Selected members panel. Finish your user assignment with clicks on Select and Assign.

Congrats Now you created trust between Azure Active Directory and Identity Authentication service.

  • IAS Tenant Final Preparation:

Navigate to Identity provisioning > Source > Jobs and run now read job to get all users from SF then schedule the job for future new hires.

Navigate to Applications and resources > Applications > SuccessFactors > Conditional Authentication and create a rule for all domains you need it to access the system from the identity provider you created... this step will define the domains witch will access as SSO, any other domain will access from the default identity provider.

Set the Default Identity Provider as Identity Authentication.

Navigate to Identity provider > Identity Federation > switch On Use Identity Authentication user store and Switch On User Access

Now you can test and be sure that the user you are try to test with is already added to the SF tenant.

Troubleshooting & Common Issues

Issue Possible Cause Solution

Users cannot log in via SSOMisconfigured SAML settingsVerify SAML assertion parameters and ACS URL
Error: "User not found"Incorrect attribute mappingEnsure Azure AD user attributes match SuccessFactors fields
Logout not working properlyLogout URL missingConfigure the logout URL in both Azure AD and SAP IAS

References & Additional Resources

  • SAP Identity Authentication Service (IAS) Documentation – Comprehensive guide on configuring IAS for SSO.

  • Azure AD SSO Configuration – Microsoft documentation on setting up SAML-based SSO in Azure AD.

  • SAP IAS Documentation – Administration Guide | PUBLIC 2025-03-05. 

By following these steps, organizations can successfully implement SSO for SAP SuccessFactors, enhancing security and user experience. If you encounter issues, refer to the troubleshooting section or consult the official documentation.

Hope you enjoy the process.

Thanks

Ahmed Aranda

entry.title: New CIO Guide: Identity Lifecycle in SAP Landscapes
entry.content:
We just published our new, comprehensive CIO Guide: Identity Lifecycle in SAP Landscapes!

This new CIO guide explores the SAP approach to identity and access management (IAM) in the context of the identity lifecycle. It explains how IAM software from SAP supports building successful system integrations in cloud and hybrid environments and includes diagrams and a reference architecture to illustrate the concepts. With SAP Cloud Identity Services and well-established IAM-related industry standards, SAP improves system integration and helps provide a seamless user experience while also improving security and compliance.

The first version of this CIO guide was released in 2018 and quickly became one of the most popular documents in the SAP security community. Despite an update of the guide in 2021, a lot has changed again since then, so we decided to issue another update that builds on the proven format while adding new technical developments and strategic recommendations.

The most important change is that we have brought the capabilities of authentication, authorization, and provisioning together into one seamless solution, SAP Cloud Identity Services. At the same time, the Identity Directory service has assumed a much more prominent role as the backbone of IAM tools and processes.

The new guide explains the identity lifecycle and the SAP Cloud Identity Services strategy and explores the SAP offerings for each area. We also introduce a section on the reference architectures for IAM to provide you with an overview and comprehensive technical diagrams for the major IAM areas of authentication, identity lifecycle, and authorization.

Read the new CIO guide!

 

For more information about SAP Cloud Identity Services and to stay up to date on the latest developments, visit our topic page in SAP Community:

https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/cloud-identity-services

entry.title: SAP IAG: Workflow Configuration for Initiator rule (Attribute based workflow)
entry.content:
In this blog, I'll walk you through configuring the workflow for the initiator rule (Attribute based workflow) in Access Request Service (IAG).

Introduction:

Different workflow paths can be applied to various types of access requests in IAG based on the attributes of the access request. This is achieved by defining the corresponding workflow business rules for Access Request Service.

Defining Business Rules:

  • If the business rules for access requests have already been defined and no different paths are required, there is no need to make any changes to those existing business rule RequestTypeRule.

 

  • If different paths are required, manually create the data objects called RequestAttributes and define a business rule called AttributesRuler. For creating the related data objects and business rules, please refer the below link.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_CLOUD_IDENTITY_ACCESS_GOVERNANCE/e12d8683adfa4471ac4edd40809b9038/13b9055585dc4d73996d9a13ab5a0b71.html?version=CLOUDFOUNDRY

 

  • Even if a new business rule, AttributesRuler, is defined and conditions are maintained within it, the RequestTypeRule serves as a fallback option. If no conditions in the AttributesRuler rule are met, the RequestTypeRule is used as the default rule to determine the appropriate path for the request. This ensures that if none of the conditions in the new rule are satisfied, the system can still fall back on the existing RequestTypeRule for path determination. 

Building a decision table for AttributesRuler:

A sample decision table for AttributesRuler can be found below.

Note: The Fixed Operator of each Condition Expression in the decision table should be “is like.” And the value of the field can be put "%" to match any values.

Explaining the decision table:

  • If the reason code selected is "New_user," the createdBy is any value, and the priority of the request is any value, the access request follows the path "accreviewpath."

 

  • Similarly, If the reason code selected is "Access_change," the createdBy is any value, and the priority of the request is any value, the access request follows the path "zmanageronlypath."

Conclusion: In conclusion, we can have distinct workflow paths for different kinds of access requests in IAG by creating the data objects called RequestAttributes, it's associated business rule AttributesRuler and the decision table.

entry.title: Update on the SAP Identity Management migration to Microsoft Entra
entry.content:
In my last blog, Preparing for SAP Identity Management’s End-of-Maintenance in 2027, I explained what SAP Identity Management customers should consider as they are preparing for a new identity lifecycle management set-up.

Our collaboration with Microsoft is progressing. The focus is on developing guidance that will enable customers to migrate their identity management scenarios from SAP Identity Management to Microsoft Entra ID. Teams on both sides are closely aligned and working on migration guidelines as well as enhancements of the respective products and services. Together with the German-speaking SAP user group DSAG and several front-runner customers, we are working on best practice guides for a successful migration.  

Microsoft recently published the first migration guideline in its Tech Community: SAP Identity Management to Microsoft Entra ID Migration Guidance Now Available. SAP customers who are using SAP Identity Management for cloud and on-premises applications like SAP SuccessFactors, SAP Cloud Identity Services, or Windows Server Active Directory can begin to use Microsoft Entra features such as Conditional Access to enforce Zero Trust access policies, and automatic provisioning that ensures users have the accounts they need for their job role. The guide offers detailed migration guidance for each SAP IDM scenario.

For SAP Identity Management customers who want more guidance on advanced IAM integration scenarios, such as Microsoft Entra ID Governance and the integration with SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance or external IDs, additional in-depth guidance for many areas will be published later this year.  

In addition, SAP just published the latest version of the CIO Guide: Identity Lifecycle in SAP Landscapes. This guide explains how identity and access management software from SAP supports building successful system integrations in cloud and hybrid environments.

entry.title: SAP Cloud IAG - Issues and Resolutions
entry.content:
Introduction: In this blog, we'll explore a few selected issues and their resolutions from a recent SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance (IAG) implementation project. This post aims to provide actionable insights to overcome similar challenges in your own IAG endeavors.

1. Provisioning of PAM ID resulting into following error:

"Privileged Access XXX_FF assignment to XYZ Failed with Invalid connector: XYY"

Resolution: Please make sure that the system which is configured in the tile “Applications” in IAG and used by PAM is created in the target system in SM59 with the same Destination name. For instance, if the application name in IAG is “S4HANA123”, then in the target system in SM59, an RFC destination of type 3 with the same name “S4HANA123” needs to be configured.

2. Error during PAM ID provisioning (java.lang.NullPointerException: cannot invoke “com.sap.conn.jco.JCoFunction.getImportParameterList()” because 'funcIAGTools” is null)

Resolution: Implement S-Note 3207285 in the backend S/4 HANA system as mentioned in the KBA ‘3238751 - IAG - PAM ID stuck in Status 'in Process'. 

3. User cannot create Access Requests & PAM Requests in IAG. Error: "Sorry, a technical error occurred. Please try again later.{ "message": "HTTP request failed", "headers": { "DataServiceVersion": "1.0", "Content-Type": "application/json", "Content-Length": "180" }, "statusCode": "403", "statusText": "Forbidden", "responseText": "{\"error\": {\"code\":\"authCheckFailed\",\"message\":{\"lang\":\"en\",\"value\":\"Logged in user and created by User are different. Therefore request content is invalid and cannot be created\"}}}" }"

Resolution: The attributes configured in IAS for the IAG application should be the only attributes as mentioned in the SAP Help documentation below.

Set Up Assertion-based Groups for IdentityAuthentication and Role Collection Mapping | SAP Help Portal

4. Approvers not receiving requests in MY inbox application

Resolution: Change the subject identifier to "User ID" in IAS for IAG application. Reference: 2929135 - IAG - Access Request is not visible in approver's Inbox

5. Email notifications are not getting triggered in IAG

Resolution: To enable the Notifications, it is required to have own SMTP server. Below 2 Destinations need to be created in the IAG Subaccount of BTP as per the instructions mentioned in KBA 3148288

a) Destination name: bpmworkflowruntime_mail

b) Destination name: parameters_destination

 6. URL links to Access Request inbox in IAG email notifications do not work.

Resolution: Please update the URL maintained in SAP BTP Cockpit, for the connector Parameters_Destination. The IAG launchpad URL format should be 'https://...hana.ondemand.com'.

Note: Do not include a trailing slash ('/') at the end of the URL.

7. Access Request Submission results in 500 Internal Server Error

Resolution: This issue is with USER ID mapping in IAG. When submitting access request, we need to ensure that the user ID matches with that of the one in target application system.

8. My Information tile is not showing User details or throwing error message "No Information available"

Resolution: Please make sure that the standard user group (IAG_USER) is created in IAS tenant and it has been assigned to users. Run the SCI user group sync in IAG once the assignment has been completed for all users in IAS. You can refer to the KBA 2790280 for more details.

Stay tuned for upcoming posts.

SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance Governance, Risk, Compliance (GRC), and Cybersecurity

entry.title: GRC, Trade, and Tax with SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2408
entry.content:
Hello and welcome to the release highlights for GRC, Trade and Tax with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408. This blog contains selected highlights from International Trade, Core Tax, SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, as well as SAP Risk and Assurance Management and SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance along with their business benefits and deep-dive system demos.

To get a quick overview of our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 highlights for GRC, Trade and Tax, watch this 3-minute video:

 

Topics of Covered in this Blog

International Trade

  • Integration of SAP Watch List Screening with Business Partner Master Data

Tax

  • AI-Assisted Configuration for US Tax Jurisdictions

  • SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Mass Processing of Reporting Tasks
  • SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Electronic Invoices in Malaysia

GRC

  • SAP Risk and Assurance Management: Automatic Issue owner Determination, Notification and Assignment

  • SAP Risk and Assurance Management: Ad-Hoc Tasks in Issue and Remediation Management

  • SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance: Enhanced Access Request Workflow

  • SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance: Fine-Grained Authorization for Privileged Access Management
  • SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance: Extension of Rule Content

 

International Trade

Integration of SAP Watch List Screening with Business Partner Master Data

SAP Watch List Screening is part of international trade compliance and checks business transactions such as sales orders and deliveries against sanctioned party lists to prevent high-risk business with sanctioned persons and organizations. As of this release, you can also screen business partners in the role of customers and suppliers. SAP Watch List Screening runs on SAP BTP and is integrated with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. ​

Value Proposition​

  • Prevent high-risk business with sanctioned persons and organizations​
  • Assure legal compliance regarding sanctioned-party-list screening for customer and supplier master data​

Capabilities​

  • Screen names and addresses of business partners in the role of customer or supplier​
  • Call the SAP Watch List Screening application on the SAP Business Technology Platform from within SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition​
  • ‘Maintain Business Partner’ app:​
  • Add status fields representing the screening status of the addresses of business partners​
  • Manage a cumulative status field on the level of general data of the business partner​
  • Consider business partner screening results during business document creation and update

 

​Video 2: As of 2408, it is now possible to screen business partners in the role of customer or supplier with SAP Watch List Screening in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

​More Information

  • SAP Help Portal: Integration with SAP Watch List Screening

Back to top

 

Tax

AI-Assisted Configuration for US Tax Jurisdictions

This feature is currently in beta testing.

Feature Overview​

If you’re using the internal UJS tax jurisdiction solution in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, you can now maintain your jurisdiction codes and rates with just three simple steps.​

  • From a dozen steps down to three: Use a natural-language prompt to retrieve detailed information from the AI, then verify, simulate, and save your configured tax jurisdiction codes and rates. ​
  • Once you’ve configured the connection between your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system and the LLM, you can chat directly with the AI to retrieve detailed address-based tax jurisdiction information. ​
  • The LLM also returns a link to the respective state tax or revenue authority, giving you an easy way to verify your tax rate information before simulating and saving your changes. 

 

​Video 3: Currently in beta testing: The AI-Assisted Configuration for US Tax Jurisdictions

Register and experience the latest AI features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and influence upcoming AI developments.

More Information

  • ​Get Involved Early

Back to top

 

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Mass Processing of Reporting Tasks

Businesses need to create reports to be submitted to the tax authorities across countries resulting in hundreds of reports per month. Instead of creating them one by one, business users can accelerate statutory reporting using the mass processing of reporting tasks in SAP Document and Reporting Compliance.

Value Proposition

  • Reduce manual effort for creation and submission of statutory reports
  • Lower the risk of noncompliance due to missed deadlines
  • Enhance the overall business user experience by reducing user interventions required during individual report processing

Capabilities

  • Mass processing of reports by allowing reporting actions such as report run creation
  • Centralized progress log indicating the overall status of the mass action as well as details of processing and results of the action for each report
  • Ability to perform report-level checks such as authorization for each report during the mass action processing
  • Parallel processing of mass actions across reports

Fig. 1: As of SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2402, business users can accelerate statutory reporting using the mass processing of reporting tasks in SAP Document and Reporting Compliance

More Information

  • SAP Help Portal: Mass Action

Back to top

 

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Electronic Invoices in Malaysia

As of August 2024, Malaysia authorities are introducing mandatory electronic invoices. The country wants to promote electronic invoicing in business transactions between businesses. Electronic invoicing is a measure for digitizing business relationships, reducing transaction costs, and increasing transparency in commercial traffic.  With SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, as of 2408, you can seamlessly generate and electronically exchange invoices through authorities’ portal.

Value Proposition

  • Enable comprehensive compliance with new regulations for electronic invoices in Malaysia
  • Deliver a consistent user experience across countries and business processes
  • Simplify implementation of new local requirements
  • Automate and simplify invoicing processes

Capabilities

  • Support validation through authorities’ portal of
  • Customer invoices
  • Buyer-issued invoices
  • Refund notes
  • Buyer rejections

Fig. 2: As of SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2402, you can seamlessly generate and electronically exchange invoices through authorities’ portal in Malaysia in SAP Document and Reporting Compliance

More Information

  • SAP Help Portal: Document and Reporting Compliance

Back to top

 

GRC

SAP Risk and Assurance Management

SAP Risk and Assurance Management is integrated with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition and provides you with the tools to become compliant with internal controls, laws, and regulations that apply to your organization. In Issue Management and Remediation, you can process and monitor issues resulting from manual or automated procedures in Issue Management and Remediation.

 

Automatic Issue Owner Determination, Notification and Assignment

Now, you benefit from automatic dispatching of new issues to issue owners, email notifications for issue nominees, and automatic issue owner assignments.

Value Proposition

  • Enable automatic dispatching of new issues to issue owners
  • Enable email notifications to make nominees aware of new issues
  • Avoid orphaned issues by automatic issue owner assignment after a grace period
  • Help issues find their best processor under consideration of absences, workload, and so on
  • Provide clear notification status for each issue available

Capabilities

  • Automatic determination of issue owner nominees based on business data
  • Email notifications with direct link to the issue for all nominees
  • Category-specific reminder email notification after a configurable period of time
  • Fallback mechanism to assign a default user after a configurable grace period
  • Dashboard provides easy access for issue nominees

More Information

  • SAP Help Portal: Notification of Nominees
  • SAP Help Portal: and How to Set Up Automatic Issue Owner Assignment (Admin Guide)

 

Video 3: Now, compliance specialists benefit from automatic dispatching of new issues to issue owners, email notifications for issue nominees, and automatic issue owner assignments in SAP Risk and Assurance Management

Back to top

 

Ad-Hoc Tasks in Issue Management and Remediation

As of this release, you can create user-defined tasks on ad-hoc basis and complement existing task lists.

Value Proposition

  • Get a flexible extension of task lists by issue owner​
  • Address topics using tasks that have not been foreseen at design time​
  • Avoid communication outside the Process Issues app in order to address issue-specific topics

Capabilities

Ad-hoc tasks allowing users to create tasks at runtime that are not provided through predefined task list templates:

  • Create user-defined tasks on an ad-hoc basis
  • Complement predefined task lists with tailored tasks

Video 4: Now, you can create user-defined tasks on ad-hoc basis and complement existing task lists in SAP Risk and Assurance Management

More Information

  • SAP Help Portal: Creating Tasks Manually

Back to top

 

SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance: Enhanced Access Request Workflow

SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance, access request service is a cloud service for creating self-service requests to applications for on-premise and cloud source applications and systems. Thanks to the new approach of a dynamic risk owner determinator, you can dynamically determine the current risk owner stage approver. This determinator assigns the risk owner stage approver based on business rules that evaluate both risk and user attributes when an access request is created.

Value Proposition

  • Gain additional flexibility to define a more streamlined access approval process based on various business needs

Capabilities

  • Additional optional configurations in the access request workflow:
  • Dynamically identify the risk approver in the approval process
  • Based on configuration setting allow access-risk mitigation during the approval process

Fig. 3: Thanks to the enhanced access request workflow, you can dynamically identify the risk approver in the access request approval process

More Information

  • SAP Help Portal: Dynamical Risk Owner Determinator

Back to top

 

SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance: Fine-Grained Authorization for Privileged Access Management

SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance, privileged access management service is a cloud solution that lets you create self-service requests for emergency access to systems and applications. Thanks to this innovation, you can facilitate granular authorization roles by application.

Value Proposition

  • Enhance security and control by helping ensure that users have access only to the applications necessary for their roles
  • Reduce the risk of unauthorized access and potential security breaches
  • Align with the principle of least privilege, and improve overall compliance with security policies and regulations

Capabilities

  • Granting access to each application within the privileged access management (PAM) service, through distinct roles in SAP Business Technology Platform, facilitating granular authorization roles

Fig. 4: Thanks to the fine-grained authorizations for Privileged Access Management, you can facilitate granular authorization roles by application

More Information

  • SAP Help Portal: Pre-delivered role collections on BTP

Back to top

 

SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance: Extension of Rule Content

SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance is integrated with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition and supports comprehensive identity management and access governance. It allows you to minimize access risk and mitigate potential risks for financial loss.

Value Proposition

  • Support continuous compliance to access policies through provision of out of the box rule set to assess access risks
  • Upgrading applications may introduce new roles, permissions, and potential conflicts. Ensures access risk rule sets are updated to maintain alignment between access risk analysis assessments and the evolving landscape.

Capabilities

  • Updated pre-delivered rule set content for additional scenarios, including:
  • Segregation of risks
  • Critical-access risks

Fig. 5: The extension of rule content ensures that access risk rule sets are updated to maintain alignment between access risk analysis assessments and the evolving landscape

More Information

  • SAP Help Portal: What’s New

Back to top 

 

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts. 

Follow us on @SAP and #S4HANA, and myself on LinkedIn and X.

📽️ Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 Early Release Series! 

From July 8 to July 12, we hosted a series of 20 compelling live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find a 60-minute-long recording and a presentation for GRC, Trade and Tax as well as 19 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand – anywhere, anytime. 

🔎Find All of Our Assets 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance Onboarding Process
entry.content:
Are you a new SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance customer or partner who is in interested in learning more about how to get started with the solution?  The SAP Onboarding Center is pleased to announce the release of a new Cloud Identity Access Governance Onboarding Resource Center and Onboarding Webcast to help you with your implementation.

Lets go through a high level of the solution and what topics are covered in these new onboarding resources.

SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance is public cloud solution from SAP's Governance, Risk and Compliance portfolio of products.  It is a leading platform that provides comprehensive security solutions for businesses.  Designed to safeguard and streamline your operations, it is currently composed of five core components: Access Analysis, Role Design, Access Request, Access Certification and Privileged Access Management.  

There are two versions of the solution:
SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance, standard edition
SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance, integration edition

If you are unsure which version your organization has purchased you can check in SAP for Me or in your SAP BTP Global Account on or after your contract start date.

How to Access
SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance is hosted on the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP).
The IT contact listed on the customers contract will receive a provisioning email notifying them that their solution is available on the SAP BTP Global Account on their contract start date. 
If you are not the IT contact on the contract and cannot access the SAP BTP Global Account you will need a Global Account Owner in your organization to grant you access to the SAP BTP Global Account. If that is not possible, view this blog to find out how to get support - [SAP BTP Onboarding Series] Initial Access to your SAP BTP Cockpit | SAP Blogs.  (If you would like to learn more about SAP BTP in general , please see this excellent series of blogs to help you get started -Introducing the SAP BTP Onboarding Blog Series - SAP Community  )

Once you have accessed the SAP BTP Global account, you can start the configuration of SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance using the guides on the SAP Help Portal.
Please note that you need to configure both a test and production tenant in the SAP BTP Cockpit.

As well as accessing SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance and doing the initial configuration, our customers and partners need to know about other topics pertaining to the cloud service, such as:

    • Integration Options
    • SAP for Me and S-User Administration
    • SAP BTP Cockpit
    • SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance Product Support
    • Cloud System Notification Subscriptions and Cloud Availability
    • Key Resources

All of these above topics and more are covered in the SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance Onboarding Webcast that is held on a quarterly basis.  If you cannot attend the live webcast, you will be sent a link to the recording or you can access the recorded session via the Onboarding Resource Center.

I look forward to working with you on your SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance onboarding journey!  Please comment if you have any questions.

Important Links:

SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance Help Portal
SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance Community
SAP Business Technology Platform Onboarding Resource Center

  • SAP Managed Tags:

entry.title: A new approach to SAP authorization roles – explained using LEGO® as an example
entry.content:
Creating and managing authorization roles in SAP S/4HANA is a complex and time-consuming process. Month-long projects that require a great deal of resource commitment are the norm. However, there is another way that significantly simplifies and accelerates this process.

A scientifically based approach to role creation

Our method for creating SAP authorization roles is based on a scientifically sound modularity that allows role building blocks to be put together flexibly and precisely. Just as LEGO® building blocks can be sorted and grouped, our approach allows authorizations to be built so that they can be aligned with the needs of the company in a consistently compliant manner, independently of the organization and company. At the same time, they must be scalable quickly and easily in order to map rollouts and changes without incurring high costs.

The technical background

The approach we developed over 15 years ago is based on the principles of system theory and modular software architecture. We chose a mathematical approach and described it in the form of an equivalence relation. We applied these findings to the SAP authorization logic from R/3 to today's S/4HANA Public Cloud, creating a way to segregate and provide roles in a highly efficient and resource-saving manner.

What makes this method special?

  • Modularity: Roles are broken down into building blocks (5-tuples) based on characteristics relevant to segregation, such as access mode, line of business, price category, business process area, and criticality, while taking into account the constraints that the number of roles must be minimal and element pairs that are on the prohibition list (SoD) must not occur in the same group. These subsets can be combined and adapted as desired, as long as they do not violate compliance, which MUST be defined.
  • Efficiency: Modularity not only reduces implementation effort by more than 80%, but also future maintenance.
  • Uniqueness: This approach is globally unique and does not use any additional tools, but simply a method based on the SAP standard in each system.

The formula as a narrative explained using LEGO® as an example

We have a huge pile of Lego bricks. They represent the functions in SAP S/4HANA ERP

We have the task of sorting all these bricks into groups according to the following rules:

  1. In each group, all bricks with the same number of stud rows (access type), the same colors (LoB), shapes (square, rectangular and round (license), sizes (business process area - SW components/package affiliation)
  2. Stones with a structured surface correspond to our criticality C5 “very critical” in separate groups
  3. Stone pairs must not be in the same group according to the definition of set V of forbidden pairs
  • Form as few groups as possible to save space and keep an overview (condition |R| is minimal)
  • Groups must be split if we find stone pairs that must not be together according to the list of prohibitions
  • Make sure that the stones with a structured surface remain separate (very high criticality)
  • Try to keep as many similar stones together as possible, as long as they are not on the forbidden list.

Result 🎯

A series of boxes (subsets) only with stones with identical properties, except for the surface finish, where the very rough or structured surface stones are separated.

Each stone is in exactly one box, and you have used as few boxes as possible.

Advantages for SAP CoE

For SAP managers, this approach means a significant reduction in project effort and thus significant cost reduction. Authorization roles are clearly structured and can be quickly adapted to changing requirements, which simplifies long-term maintenance. And they are compliant!

Discussion and exchange

Perhaps there are other approaches that redefine the limits of what is possible and make the seemingly impossible possible. I would be interested to hear about them. I welcome any exchange of ideas. I learn by listening and sharing knowledge.

If you like, you can watch the video, which explains the method on a slightly less technical level: Is LEGO® revolutionizing the SAP S/4HANA authorization chaos?

Note: translated with DeepL

entry.title: Title: S/4HANA authorization management: the SAP Activate method thought differently
entry.content:
The implementation or redesign of SAP authorizations is often associated with long projects, complex requirements and high resource expenditure. Especially in times of crisis, the feeling of uncertainty when implementing such projects can be overwhelming. But does it really have to be like this?

In the wake of the current economic uncertainty, I have developed a completely new approach - the Rapid System Implementation (RSI) method ‘4i’. This method is not only faster, but also eliminates many of the classic project risks. How does it work? By using established best practices in a way that minimises risks and drastically shortens the project duration.

Why is the crisis the right time for innovation?

Crises always mean opportunities! Richard David Precht summed it up perfectly: ‘If there is an opportunity to rethink, then there is an opportunity to rethink in a crisis’. For us, this means that especially in difficult economic times, the time has come to rethink old patterns and break new ground - even in the S/4HANA authorization world.

The RSI 4i method - a new approach for SAP authorization projects

The Rapid System Implementation 4i method is the key to a fast, secure and flexible implementation of SAP authorizations. Here are the four steps that make up this approach:

  1. information: structured keycards are used to collect all relevant company information in order to optimally customize the SAP best practice building blocks.
  2. installation: The best practice package is installed immediately at all levels - including individual roles, catalogues and business roles.
  3. integration: Short test phases by key users ensure that everything works smoothly before we go live.
  4. implementation: SOD-compliant roles are assigned in stages and without risk - thanks to the option of secure fallback.

What's special about this method?

  • Speed: The project timeframe is drastically reduced and we achieve the goals faster than ever before.
  • Security: The RSI method eliminates many of the classic project risks. As we do not touch the old authorizations, there is always a fallback.
  • Efficiency: The introduction of best-practice roles is structured and without the typical lengthy problems that often occur in complex projects.

Why SAP_BR* roles are not the answer

An important note: The SAP_BR* roles supplied by SAP are not intended for productive use (see Administration Guide to Implementation of SAP S/4HANA, SAP Help etc.). These roles are intended for demo purposes only and do not fulfil any regulatory requirements. An individually adapted authorization concept is therefore essential - and this is precisely where we come in with the best practice approach using the RSI 4i method.

Conclusion

It is possible to think differently about authorization projects and reach the goal faster - without the usual project pain. The RSI 4i method offers a real alternative that combines efficiency, security and speed.

If you too would like to think and act differently during the crisis, I invite you to a strategy meeting. Together we can discuss your individual challenges and identify concrete solutions.

Discussion & exchange

I welcome your feedback and look forward to hearing your ideas and exchanging experiences on SAP authorizations - thinking differently.

If you like, you are welcome to watch the video, which explains the method on a slightly less technical level: S/4HANA authorization management thought differently

 

Note: translated with DeepL

entry.title: SAP IAG Access Control Mail Notification SMTP Configuration using Onprem Exchange Mail Server
entry.content:
Hi Community of SAP,

In this article, you will learn how to configure SMTP mail notifications using an on-prem Exchange Mail Server for SAP Identity Access Governance (IAG). It provides a step-by-step guide to ensure that your system sends email notifications correctly and securely, making it a valuable resource for SAP administrators and consultants.

You can review the SAP note below to connect Office 365 and similar cloud products:
3148288 - Email is not getting triggered in IAG 

Thanks for your support for this process. Alican Atik (GRC Consultant), Anıl Kırlı (GRC Consultant), Katalin Joo (SAP Support), @UlasCan Bozkurt (Basis Consultant)

 

  1. Configure on Cloud Connector.

    Add new mapping item on Cloud to Onpremise.

    Backend Type: Non-Sap System
    Protocol: TCP

    Enter Internal Host you want to access.

    Enter Virtual Host. The Virtual Host must be different from Internal Host.


    Your configuration should be looks like.

  2. Get Base64 certificate from mail server. (You can ask customer or your local IT side)
  3. Configure on BTP Destinations.

    Name: bpmworkflowruntime_mail
    Type: MAIL
    Description: mail
    Proxy Type: Internet
    Authentication: BasicAuthentication
    User: *Your SMTP User*
    Password: *Your SMTP User Password*

    Additional Properties
    mail.bpm.send.disabled: false
    mail.mode: CloudConnector
    mail.server_cert: *Your Mail Server Base64 Certificate*
    mail.smtp.auth: true
    mail.smtp.from: *Your SMTP User Mail*
    mail.smtp.host: *Your Virtual Host as you configured on Cloud Connector.*
    mail.smtp.port: *Your port as you configured on Cloud Connector.*
    mail.smtp.ssl.checkserveridentity: true
    mail.smtp.starttls.enable: true
    mail.smtp.starttls.required: true
    mail.transport.protocol: smtp

    Should be looks like.

Important notes:
1. Make sure about your Cloud Connector is up to date.
2. BASE64 Certificate is long text. You need to copy and paste to mail.server_cert without *BEGIN CERTIFICATE* lines.
3. We struggled a lot when this data was not available from any source. If you get an error, the log is only available on the SAP side. You can open a ticket to SAP and get the details of the error.


Conclusion

In this blog post, we have explored the configuration process for setting up SMTP mail notifications in SAP Identity Access Governance (IAG). By following the steps outlined, users can ensure that their SAP system is properly configured to send automated notifications via an on-premise Exchange Server. This configuration is essential for keeping users informed of critical access control events in real-time, enhancing both security and compliance.

I hope this guide provides clear and actionable insights into streamlining your SAP IAG notification process.

Thanks.
Mehmet Said Demir

entry.title: Master S/4HANA authorisation issues: A practical guide with Visio Overview
entry.content:
Master S/4HANA authorization issues: A practical guide with Visio overview

Authorization issues in SAP S/4HANA can significantly impact your day-to-day work. As an SAP authorization expert, I would like to present a systematic troubleshooting overview that may help you to efficiently overcome these challenges.

The challenge

SAP authorizations are as diverse as the business processes they protect. Small mistakes can have far-reaching consequences. The trick is to develop a flexible but clearly regulated authorisation concept that can adapt to changing requirements without becoming inconsistent. The world of authorizations has not become any easier with S/4HANA.

A systematic approach to troubleshooting

To effectively solve authorization problems, I have created an overview that structures the troubleshooting process from the client level to the backend system. Let's go through the key steps:

1. client level (Fiori Launchpad)

  • Fiori Launchpad App Support: Provides important information such as the SAP Fiori ID, business server page name, service node and OData service, as well as any authorisation, gateway or runtime errors that have occurred.
  • Browser Developer Tools (F12):
    • Elements: Check the loaded components
    • Console: JavaScript errors and runtime problems
    • Sources: Direct access to JavaScript code Custom code file debugging
    • Network: Checking the OData HTTP URLs Requests, responses and error messages

2. Front-End Server (FES)

  • /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE (OData configuration):
    • Model cache cleanup
    • Metadata reload
    • SICF configuration
    • Service addition and configuration
    • Gateway client: Cache cleanup, cache rebuild, request execution
    • Service Implementation: Checking for errors or insufficient authorizations
    • RFC Connection (SM59): Ensuring the correct configuration between the front end and the back end
  • /IWFND/ERROR_LOG (OData Error Log): Analysing data transfer problems between FES and BES

3. Back-End Server (BES)

  • STAUTHTRACE: Detailed analysis of authorization problems, including CDS view level
  • FLP Content Manager (/UI2/FLPCM_CUST): Checking the Fiori catalogues and their configuration
  • PFCGUPDATEROLEMENU: Matching the catalogues with authorization roles
  • Profile generation and user matching: Ensuring consistency

Fiori Apps Library: An indispensable tool

The Fiori Apps Library is invaluable at every stage of the analysis. It provides detailed information on prerequisites, installation requirements, component statuses and service configurations for each Fiori app.

Conclusion

This structured approach makes it possible to systematically isolate and resolve authorization problems. Note that the order of the steps may vary depending on the specific error. Experience plays an important role, but even for experienced experts, this overview can serve as a valuable checklist.

I invite you to download the S/4HANA authorization troubleshooting overview and use it in your daily work. Feedback and suggestions for improvement are explicitly encouraged, as we can all learn from each other.

Do you have any questions or comments? I look forward to your comments and a lively exchange!

 

Note: translated with DeepL

entry.title: SAP Cloud Identity Services - IPS ( How to Assign default groups and Unassign groups on Condition)
entry.content:
Managing user groups efficiently is crucial for maintaining security and ensuring that users have the appropriate access within your organization. SAP Cloud Identity Services, specifically the Identity Provisioning Service (IPS), offers robust capabilities to automate and streamline this process. In this blog post, we’ll explore how to assign default groups to users and unassign groups based on specific conditions using IPS Transformtion logic.

Below is the scenario or example

Assigning Default Groups

Assigning default groups to users can be essential for ensuring that active users have the necessary permissions from the moment they are onboarded or access to the certain applications.

Here’s a step-by-step guide to achieve this:

  1. Assign "DefaultGroup" in IPS Configuration:
    Navigate to the Identity Services - Identity Provisioning tab and to your Target System.
    Under the Transformation tab, modify according to your condition in transformation rule.

    Note : below JSON is sample with having 1 group

 

{
  "condition": "$.userType == 'employee'",
  "constant": "DefaultGroup",
  "targetPath": "$.groups[0].value"
}

 

Note : below JSON is sample which is good practice to define your multiple groups having Target Variable

 

            {
                "condition": "$.active == true",
                "constant": [
                    {
                        "id": "SAP Build Apps"
                    },
                    {
                        "id": "SAP Build Code"
                    }
                ],
                "targetVariable": "assignGroup"
            },
            {
                "condition": "$.active == false",
                "constant": [
                    {
                        "id": "SAP Build Apps"
                    },
                    {
                        "id": "SAP Build Code"
                    }
                ],
                "targetVariable": "unassignGroup"
            }

 

Apply the Transformation:

  • Save the transformation rule and apply
  • Go to the relevant source system.
  • Run the provisioning job to ensure that all active users are assigned to the default group upon creation/modification and Inactive Users are unassigned from the default group defined.

Best Practices

  • Regularly Review and Update Rules: Ensure that your transformation rules are up-to-date with your organization’s policies and structure.
  • Test in a Sandbox Environment: Before applying any new rules to your production environment, test them in a sandbox to avoid unintended access issues.
  • Monitor Provisioning Jobs: Regularly monitor the results of your provisioning jobs to ensure that groups are being assigned and unassigned as expected.

By leveraging the capabilities of SAP Cloud Identity Services - IPS, you can automate the management of user groups, ensuring that your users always have the appropriate access based on their roles and conditions. This not only enhances security but also improves operational efficiency.

entry.title: Enabling Authorization Policies in SAP Cloud Identity Services - IAS with AMS/CAP
entry.content:
SAP Cloud Identity Services provide a robust framework for managing user identities and authorizations across various SAP applications. One of the key components of this framework is the Identity Authentication Service (IAS), which integrates seamlessly with the Authorization Management Service (AMS) and the Cloud Application Programming (CAP) model. This blog will guide you through the steps to enable authorization policies using these services.

Understanding the Components

1. Identity Authentication Service (IAS): IAS is a cloud service that provides secure authentication for users accessing SAP applications. It supports various authentication methods, including single sign-on (SSO), and integrates with other identity providers.

2. Authorization Management Service (AMS): AMS is a centralized service for managing authorizations across SAP applications. It allows administrators to define and enforce authorization policies, ensuring that users have the appropriate access rights.

3. Cloud Application Programming (CAP) Model: CAP is a framework for building enterprise-grade applications on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). It simplifies the development process by providing a set of tools and libraries for building, deploying, and managing applications.

Steps to Enable Authorization Policies

  1. Access SAP IAS Admin Console:

    • Log in to the SAP IAS Admin console through the SAP BTP cockpit - SAP Cloud Identity Services.
    • Navigate to the “Application & Resources” and "Tenant Settings" section.
    • Follow the Screenshot 1

  2. Enable Policy Based Authorization:

    • Click on Policy Based Authorizations and enable toggle button to activate the services
    • Follow the Screenshot 2

  3. Validate Authorization Policies visible:

    • Navigate back to "Applications" and Search for "Admin"
    • Under "System Applications" - Click on Administration Console
    • Check you will see additional tab - "Authorization Policies"
    • Follow the Screenshot 3

Screenshot 1

Screenshot 2

Screenshot 3

Step 3: Integrate AMS with CAP Applications

  1. Add AMS Dependencies:

    • In your CAP project, add the necessary AMS dependencies to your package.json or pom.xml file. For example:
      JSON
      "dependencies": {
  "@sap/ams-dev": "^2.0.0",
  "@sap/cds-dk": "^8.6.0"
}

  2. Configure Security in CAP:

    • Update your CAP application’s security configuration to integrate with AMS. This involves setting up JWT decoders and security filters to handle authorization tokens.
      Java
       
      @Configuration
@EnableWebSecurity
@Profile("cloud")
@Order(1)
public class SecurityConfig {
    @Autowired
    private JwtDecoder jwtDecoder;

    @Bean
    public SecurityFilterChain securityFilterChain(HttpSecurity http) throws Exception {
        http.authorizeRequests()
            .antMatchers("/public/**").permitAll()
            .anyRequest().authenticated()
            .and()
            .oauth2ResourceServer()
            .jwt()
            .decoder(jwtDecoder);
        return http.build();
    }
}

  3. Deploy and Test:

    • Deploy your CAP application to SAP BTP and test the authorization policies. Ensure that users can only access the resources they are authorized to


Conclusion

By following these steps, you can effectively enable and manage authorization policies in SAP Cloud Identity Services using IAS, AMS, and the CAP model. This setup ensures secure and efficient access control across your SAP applications, enhancing both security and user experience.

Feel free to reach out if you have any questions or need further assistance with your SAP Cloud Identity Services setup!

I hope this blog helps you get started with enabling authorization policies in SAP Cloud Identity Services. If you have any specific questions or need further details, let me know!

entry.title: Adapting to a New Reality: SAP’s Access Governance Solutions
entry.content:
With the rise of AI, cloud adoption, and stricter compliance demands, access governance—especially with fine-grained Segregation of Duties (SoD) analysis—has become essential. Industries such as finance, healthcare, and government face strict regulations (e.g., GDPR, SOX, HIPAA) that require safeguarding sensitive data and controlling user access. Access governance solutions are vital in ensuring compliance and security, reducing the risk of fraud, errors, and data breaches. 

Unauthorized access remains a primary cause of data breaches, leading to significant financial and reputational damage. A 2024 Ponemon/ IBM report highlighted that the average cost of a data breach is $4.45 million. By implementing SoD analysis, businesses can mitigate such risks by ensuring that critical tasks are distributed among multiple individuals, preventing potential fraud or mismanagement. 

SAP customers are in the process of moving from on-premises as well as implementing new cloud solutions to take advantage of the benefits they offer. Access governance solutions are required as critical in each of these transformations in all geographic areas, industries, and organizational sizes. To support these varied customer adoption challenges, SAP offers two key solutions for access governance: SAP Access Control (SAP AC) and SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance (SAP Cloud IAG). These solutions cater to different business requirements, technology stacks, and implementation models. 

 

SAP Access Control: Flexible and Scalable for Large Enterprises  

SAP AC is ideal for large organizations with complex on-premises systems and hybrid environments. AC is available in both on-premise and private cloud deployment models. SAP Access Control has a combination of configuration options, APIs, and interfaces that enable extensive customization. The customization applies to each module and access governance process. Access Control has been implemented across SAP’s largest customers with broad application landscapes.

SAP Access Control Features 

  • Seamless Cloud Migration: SAP AC preserves custom configurations during cloud transitions, ensuring continued security and compliance. 
  • Fine-Grained SoD for Legacy & Modern Systems: SAP AC applies flexible SoD controls across both legacy and modern cloud-based systems. 
  • Compliance & Risk Management: It helps businesses manage SoD violations, ensuring adherence to regulations like SOX and GDPR. 
  • Flexible workflow engine – Based on SAP Business Workflow, this is highly flexible and customizable to handle different user provisioning and approval requirements. 
  • Extensive reporting – The solution offers a wide set out of the box reporting out of the box based on secured audit certifiable data.  
  • Emergency Access Management – Access Control includes a complete end-to-end solution for assigning temporary elevated permissions for urgent situations, complete with activity monitoring and post-session reviews.

 

SAP Cloud IAG: Designed for cloud - streamlined and optimized.  

SAP IAG is a cost-effective Saas solution that supports both on-premise and cloud applications with complete functionality and fast ROI. SAP Cloud IAG includes pre-configured templates, rulesets, and workflows with a simplified implementation process. IAG is built from the ground up on a multi-tenant Saas solution running on BTP. There is less to configure and once subscribed, customers get two tenants ready to run. All that’s necessary is to connect IAG with the business applications.  

There are two versions of SAP IAG – standard and integration edition. The standard edition supports full functionality while the integration edition is tailored as a cloud application extension for SAP Access Control. 

  • Faster Implementation: SAP Cloud IAG reduces deployment time with out-of-the-box access risk ruleset content and persona-based navigation. 
  • Scalability Across Hybrid Environments: It supports both cloud and on-premises environments, making it suitable for hybrid or multi-cloud strategies. 
  • Third-Party Integration: SAP Cloud IAG integrates with a broad range of third-party applications, ensuring consistent access governance across the entire IT landscape. 
  • Integrates/Extends Access Control – IAG, integration edition provides cloud system support for SAP Access Control. 
  • S4 Hana Public Cloud – The only access governance solution for SAP S4 Hana Public Cloud.
  • SAP Cloud Identity Services – Seamless integration with SAP Cloud Identity Services for security and broad integration with SAP enterprise applications.                       
 

Conclusion: Securing the Digital Future with SAP Access Governance 

With SAP's comprehensive solutions, SAP Access Control and SAP Cloud IAG, organizations can ensure secure, compliant, and efficient management of user access across both legacy and cloud systems. Whether it's large enterprises with complex hybrid environments or businesses adopting cloud-first strategies, SAP provides the tools needed to protect sensitive data, reduce access risk, and maintain regulatory compliance. By leveraging the right access governance tools, businesses can safeguard their operations and build trust in an increasingly interconnected digital landscape. 

 

More information  

  • SAP Access Control Product Page and Documentation 
  • SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance Product Page and Documentation

entry.title: SAP Cloud Identity Services - SAML 2.0 Automatic Certificate Renewal [metadata URL is must]
entry.content:
Here's how it works:

  1. First Sign-In Attempt: When a user attempts to sign in and the Identity Authentication detects that the encryption certificate or SAML 2.0 certificate has expired, the sign-in attempt fails.
  2. Automatic Update: The identity authentication system immediately retrieves the metadata URL provided and uses it to update the expired certificates.
  3. Successful Sign-In: Once the certificates are updated, the user can successfully sign in, ensuring uninterrupted access to digital resources.

Metadata URL is must for automatic Renewal of signing and encryption certificates of the application

This automatic update feature offers several benefits:

  • Enhanced Security: By ensuring that certificates are always current, the system prevents potential security breaches that could arise from expired certificates.
  • Improved User Experience: Users experience minimal disruption, as the system handles certificate updates seamlessly in the background.
  • Reduced Administrative Burden: IT administrators no longer need to manually track and update certificates, freeing them to focus on other critical tasks.

In conclusion, the ability to automatically update expired encryption certificates and SAML 2.0 certificates is a game-changer in the realm of identity authentication. It enhances security, improves user experience, and reduces the administrative burden, making it an essential feature for any modern digital infrastructure.

entry.title: Central Store-Based Provisioning: The Ultimate Solution for Application User Sync from IPS 🔁
entry.content:
Central store-based provisioning enables the automatic provisioning of application-specific groups from the Identity Directory to the target systems whenever changes occur. These changes include user assignments or modifications of group attributes.

Note

Currently, application-specific groups are supported for 

  • SAP Advanced Financial Closing
  • SAP Ariba Applications
  • SAP Analytics Cloud
  • SAP Application Server ABAP
  • SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management
  • SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud
  • Microsoft Entra ID
  • Local Identity Directory provisioning systems

Create Application-Specific Groups or Assign Application-Specific Groups to existing Group

you can create application-specific groups in the Identity Directory of your SAP Cloud Identity Services tenant and provision them afterward to target systems of your choice.

Operating with application-specific groups by Identity Provisioning service requires having a source system with set property ips.application.id. By running provisioning jobs from such source system you can create, update, and delete application-specific groups in the Identity Directory of your SAP Cloud Identity Services tenant, depending on the values of their attributes.

Enable or Disable Central Store-Based Provisioning

You can enable or disable the Central Store-Based Provisioning option in the administration console for SAP Cloud Identity Services.

  • Under Applications and Resources, choose the Applications tile.
  • Choose the application that you want
  • Under the Provisioning tab, enable or disable the Central Store-Based Provisioning option
  • Once the application has been updated, the system displays the message Application <name of application> updated.

When you enable the Central Store-Based Provisioning for a specific application, whenever you update an application-specific group associated with this application, a provisioning of the updates is triggered, there is no need to run manual or scheduled jobs in the Identity Provisioning

IPS Transformation Changes

Select the relevant source system, open the Properties tab, choose Edit and add the property ips.application.id =6f187cce-2f51-4efd-9bf4-9a8aabdd1c9cMonitor Central Store Logs

Central Store Logs provide information about the application-specific groups that have been provisioned from the Identity Directory to your target systems.

Note

Central Store Logs are enabled and will appear under Provisioning Logs whenever changes to application-specific groups occur in the Identity Directory, such as assigning users or modifying group attributes.

 select your Identity Provisioning --->  Provisioning Logs --->  Central Store Logs

entry.title: Step-by-Step Guide: Adding Your Domain to Trusted Domains in SAP IAS
entry.content:
When using embedded iframes or overlays in an application, which authenticates against the Identity Authentication service, the error detailed in the screenshot below is displayed when trying to log in via the domain of the overlay.

Possible error messages  are displayed:

  • "Your domain is not trusted. Please contact your system administrator."
  • "Your request failed because the page you have to be redirected to is not trusted. Please contact your system administrator."

To fix issue - Configure Trusted Domains

Kindly ask your SAP IAS Admin to follow the below steps to fix the issue

  1. Add the necessary domain(s) to the IAS tenant's trust settings as described in Configure Trusted Domains
  2. Enable third-party cookies in the browser (check the respective browser's documentation);
  3. Disable "Prevent cross-site tracking" or "Do not track" options in the browser (the terminology may vary; check the respective browser's documentation).

entry.title: SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance, Integration Edition: Configuration Settings and Implementation
entry.content:
This blog addresses common questions about configuration settings and best practices for SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance, integration edition. It offers guidance on detecting and resolving configuration issues, adopting best practices, and utilizing helpful tips to enhance efficiency and ensure a seamless implementation.

SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance Overview

Access Analysis: Analyze access to identify potential risks, such as excessive or unauthorized access, and to ensure compliance with regulatory requirements by either refining user assignments or manage controls.

Role Design: Optimize process of defining, creating, and managing business roles within an organization to include cloud access and streamline governance processes.

Access Request: Formalized and optimized procedure for submitting, reviewing, and approving or denying access assignment requests. It ensures that only authorized and approved individuals can get provisioned to target applications and access what they are intended to, helping organizations maintain security and compliance. 

Privileged Access Management: Manage, control and monitor access to critical systems, data, and applications by users with elevated permissions by implementing strict controls and audit trails to prevent misuse or breaches.

 

Implementation Prerequisites for SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance in integration (Bridge) Mode with SAP Access Control

Connector Prerequisites:

  • In SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance:
    • Connect only one Access Control System per instance.
    • Follow KBA 3561030 (IAG-Bridge: Pre-requisites Jobs for Bridge Setup) for the sequence of jobs to be executed.
  • In SAP Access Control:
    • Ensure connector names in SAP Access Control match those in SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance.
    • Align Business Function Group and Connector Group names across both systems.
    • Include the cloud connector created in SAP Access Control within the connector group.

 

Rulesets and Mitigation Control Integration Prerequisites:

  • SAP Access Control mitigation approvers and monitors should match SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance groups.
    • Before synchronizing rules and mitigation controls from SAP Access Control to SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance, it is necessary to maintain the mapping of Access Control mitigation owners to SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance owner groups. This mapping should be updated in SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance under "Configuration" --> "Approver Mapping" whenever new owner and monitor groups are created.

 

Character Restrictions for Entities (Risk ID, Mitigation Control ID, Connector/Application):

Connectors and Connector Groups:

  • Connector: Ensure the name is limited to 20 characters as SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance only allows a maximum of 20 characters for the Application name.
  • Connector Group: Limit names to 10 characters, as the AC Connector Group in SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance restricts business function group names to this length.

Mitigation Control:

  • In SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance, controls should adhere to a 10-character limit. Maintain this length for controls integrated from other systems.

Risk:

  • SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance does not use rule IDs; therefore, risk identifiers should be limited to 8 characters.
  • The AC Target connector will not be displayed on the application tile in IAG.
  • Risks created in AC against a connector will be displayed in IAG against a Logical Group, named as connectorname_LG.

 

Best Practices:

  • Rule Set Synchronization: Exporting and importing rule sets directly from SAP Access Control to SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance is not supported. Instead, use the synchronization job, which includes the necessary mapping and conversion of the rule set format for IAG, rather than relying on manual uploads or downloads.
  • Mitigating Control Assignments: Mass mitigating control assignments for users or roles should only be conducted within SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance to ensure accurate access risk analysis results. Mass maintenance actions in Access Control may not synchronize correctly with IAG.
  • Workflow Management: Disable the mitigation control assignment approval workflow when the BRIDGE scenario is active, as the control assignments are managed directly in SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance.
  • Access Synchronization in Business Role Management: To ensure SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance accesses are available in SAP Access Control Business Role Management, utilize the ‘SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance Role Import’ option during repository synchronization jobs for cloud connectors. Subsequent updates to access attributes such as business process, criticality, and approvers must be maintained in SAP Access Control. Do not use the ‘Role Import’ utility from the NWBC launchpad for cloud access.

 

Troubleshooting Tips and Tricks 

When encountering issues categorized below, please proceed as follows:

  1. Job Logs Show Connectivity Errors in IAG Synchronization Jobs:
    • Cloud Connectivity Certificates: Certificates have a finite validity period. Check the cloud connectivity certificates of the subaccount when you encounter job log errors related to communication. Certificates should be updated before they expire. Configure alerts for expired certificates or when the cloud connector is down: Alerting | SAP Help Portal.
    • RFC User Configuration: Communication errors can also be due to issues with the RFC user configuration in the SAP BTP account destination of SAP Access Control. Ensure that RFC user accounts are not locked, expired, and that their passwords are updated in accordance with organizational policies. The RFC user must have proper authorizations as outlined in the SAP Help security guide.
  2. Missing Risk Descriptions and Levels in SAP Access Control:
    • Ensure any new risks and functions defined in SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance are synchronized to SAP Access Control via a repository synchronization job to your SAP Access Control system.
  3. Provisioning Status for Cloud Provisioning Events Not Updated in SAP Access Control Audit Log:
    • Verify Connectivity:
      • Use the IAGProvisionStatusUpdate service in BTP to run the "check connection" test. A successful test will result in a proper connection, as demonstrated below in the image.
      • If the connectivity test fails, check that the service IAG_PROVISION_STATUS_UPDATE_SRV is operational in SAP Access Control. Ensure the configuration settings match those highlighted in green below. If the settings are incorrect or the service is unavailable, contact your basis team to review the Cloud Connector settings. Install Connector | SAP Help Portal.
    • Service Check in SAP Gateway Client:
      • Ensure that the test is successfully completed in the SAP Gateway Client.
      • Verify that SSL is enabled for the IAG_PROVISION_STATUS_UPDATE_SRV service in SAP Access Control. The SAP Gateway Client should be enabled as outlined in the SAP Help document: Create Destinations for SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance Service | SAP Help Portal.
  4. Troubleshooting Repository Synchronization Failures in SAP Access Control:
    • Test Connections:
      • Use transaction SM59 to test connections for IAG_SOD_AUTH or BRIDGE_SOD_AUTH. If you encounter 201 or 500 errors during the SM59 Connection Test, this typically indicates an expired user password.
      • To resolve 201 or 500 errors, update the user password in the SAP Identity Authentication Service (IAS). For guidance on configuring IAS user authentication, refer to the SAP Help document: Create Authorization Credentials | SAP Help Portal.
    • Firewall Configuration Issues:
      • If the connection test results in not reachable or NIECONN_REFUSED (-10) errors, these may be due to firewall configuration issues.
      • To address this, use the following to identify the IP addresses needed for the correct firewall port configuration settings: Regions and API Endpoints | SAP Help Portal.
  5. ‘System XXX Cannot be Connected’ Error in Access Control:
    • Refer to the knowledge base article for resolving connectivity issues related to the system XXX in the context of IAG - Error: System XXX cannot be connected (Bridge) | HCSM.
  6. HTTP Error 500 During Repository Synchronization Job:
    • For errors during repository synchronization for any cloud connector, consult the relevant KBA: Repository Sync throwing HTTP Error500 for cloud systems in IAG Bridge | HCSM.

entry.title: Advantages of Proactive SAP IDM Migration
entry.content:
Preparing for SAP IDM’s End of Maintenance: Why Proactive Action Matters

SAP Identity Management (IDM) has long been a cornerstone of many organizations' Identity and Access Management (IAM) strategies. However, with the official end of maintenance set for 2027 (and extended maintenance only available until 2030), the clock is ticking for businesses relying on this solution. It’s critical to act now and start planning your journey to transition to another IDM system. Delaying the process could lead to unforeseen risks, such as compliance violations, operational disruptions, or increased migration costs.
Proactive migration allows you to mitigate these risks while seizing the opportunity to modernize and future-proof your IAM ecosystem. With a structured approach, you can transform this necessity into a strategic advantage.

 

Re-Engineering Your IAM Processes: A Unique Opportunity

Migrating from SAP IDM isn’t just about replacing one tool with another - it’s a chance to revolutionize your approach to identity and access management. Here’s why this transition offers unparalleled potential for improvement:

  • Redefine your IAM Requirements: Take this opportunity to collect updated business needs and redefine your identity management requirements.
  • Adapt to Hybrid or Cloud-Only Landscapes: Modernize your IAM processes to meet the demands of hybrid and cloud-first environments, ensuring flexibility and scalability.
  • Overcome Current Product Limitations: Address and eliminate process constraints imposed by your SAP IDM system, unlocking new possibilities.
  • Incorporate AI Capabilities: Integrate artificial intelligence into your IAM to enhance security, streamline operations, and automate decision-making.
  • Leverage the SAP Toolchain: Utilize powerful tools like LeanIX and Signavio to gain transparency, optimize processes, and ensure smooth integration throughout your migration.

The SAP IDM migration project is your chance to align IAM processes with current and future business strategies, ensuring your organization stays ahead in an ever-evolving digital landscape.

SAP IAM Reference Architecture (Source: SAP Discovery Center)

 

Expert Support for a Successful Transition

SAP Professional Services offers tailored guidance to help you migrate from SAP IDM effectively - the SAP IDM Migration Coach. Our offerings includes:

  • Expert coaching to navigate your migration journey with confidence.
  • Support for seamless integration of your next IAM solution.
  • Collaboration with Microsoft for Entra ID-based migrations.

Business outcomes with SAP Professional Services, you’ll achieve:

  • A smooth migration process, ready for future integration and compliance needs.
  • AI-compatible, future-resilient IAM systems built for the cloud.
  • Enhanced transparency and optimized user management using SAP tools like Signavio and LeanIX.

 

Get Started Today

Ready to embark on your SAP IDM migration journey? Find out more about the offering in SAP IDM Migration Coach - One Pager and get in contact with SAP Professional Services today to learn more about how our expert team can assist you in achieving a seamless and successful transition. Reach out to us and take the first step towards a future-ready Identity Management System.

 

Related Topics

Preparing for SAP Identity Management’s End-of-Maintenance in 2027 (blog)

Migrate identity management scenarios from SAP IDM to Microsoft Entra (learning)
